summary: Azure VPN Gateway sends encrypted traffic between Azure virtual networks and on-premises locations over the public internet using IPsec/IKE tunnels. It supports Site-to-Site, Point-to-Site, and VNet-to-VNet connection types. Multiple connections share available gateway bandwidth, and gateways can be deployed zone-redundantly for higher availability.

key_concepts.explanation: IPsec/IKE VPN tunnel between VPN Gateway and an on-premises VPN device; used for persistent cross-premises connectivity
VPN connection from an individual remote client to Azure VNet using SSTP, IKEv2, or OpenVPN; used for remote workers
IPsec/IKE tunnel between two Azure VPN gateways in different VNets, even across regions or subscriptions
Determines throughput, max tunnels, BGP support, and zone-redundancy; ranges from Basic (100 Mbps) to VpnGw5 (10 Gbps)
PolicyBased uses static routing (legacy, limited features); RouteBased uses dynamic routing and is required for P2S, VNet-to-VNet, and BGP
Active-active uses two public IPs for higher availability; active-passive keeps one gateway in standby
Deploying with AZ SKU (e.g., VpnGw1AZ) spreads across availability zones, protecting against zone-level failures
VPN Gateway can act as a failover path for ExpressRoute or connect sites not on ExpressRoute

exam_focus_points: Know which connection type to use: S2S for branch offices, P2S for remote users, VNet-to-VNet for inter-VNet connectivity
Basic SKU does NOT support BGP, zone-redundancy, or IKEv2/OpenVPN for P2S — it is dev/test only
PolicyBased gateways only support S2S with static routing; RouteBased supports all connection types
If you need more than 100 S2S tunnels, use Virtual WAN, not VPN Gateway
VPN Gateway is billed on two dimensions: hourly compute (by SKU) and egress data transfer
Zone-redundant SKUs have 'AZ' suffix (e.g., VpnGw1AZ) and require a specific public IP SKU (Standard, static)
VPN Gateway requires a GatewaySubnet in the VNet — this subnet cannot be used for other resources
Bandwidth is shared across all tunnels connected to the same gateway
common_mistakes: Confusing VNet Peering with VPN Gateway — peering is non-encrypted and lower latency; VPN Gateway encrypts traffic and works over the internet
Choosing Basic SKU for production — it lacks BGP, zone-redundancy, and IKEv2/OpenVPN support
Assuming VPN Gateway and ExpressRoute are mutually exclusive — they can coexist with VPN as failover
Forgetting that all tunnels share gateway bandwidth — one high-traffic tunnel can starve others
Confusing PolicyBased (static, legacy) with RouteBased (dynamic) — most modern features require RouteBased
Not accounting for the GatewaySubnet requirement — it must be named exactly 'GatewaySubnet'
